findings.evidence: `rg` shows the new theorem at `code/TauCeti/Probability/PathSpace/Shift.lean:64` and the existing helper at `code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/Contractability.lean:134`: `((pathLaw μ X).map (fun x : ℕ → α => fun k => x (φ k))).map (prefixProj α n) = blockLaw μ X (fun i : Fin n => φ i.val)`.
findings.fix: Expose or move the generic `map_reindex_prefixProj_pathLaw` helper and prove/use this case from it after `shift_iterate_apply`/`Measure.map_map` rewriting, or remove the shift-specific theorem.
findings.issue: `map_prefixProj_shift_iterate_pathLaw` is the `φ k = k + r` special case of the existing reindexed-prefix path-law lemma, creating a parallel shift-only finite-block API.

summary: The PR still adds a shift-specific finite-block path-law theorem that overlaps TauCeti's existing generic reindexing helper instead of reusing or exposing the general API.
